reason: Correct the public model from generic VAT claims to the official Comoros Taxe sur la Consommation contract and gate every special Article 152 rate by evidence.

officialSources.title: Ministry report on 2024 indirect-tax expenditures, published 2026
officialSources.evidence: Taxe sur la Consommation reference rate 10%; official warning that indirect-tax law requires updating.

officialSources.title: Official DGI Code general des impots 2023

separateQuantityTax.reason: Article 152 treats this as a separate additional quantity-based tax; the public calculator and AfroVAT KM endpoint accept amounts, not call minutes.

failClosedRules: No broad food, medical, education, export or agriculture shortcut.
Every non-10% treatment requires rateEvidenceConfirmed=true and the exact Article 152 evidence type in both the browser engine and AfroVAT KM request validator.
The 0% treatment requires the exact ministerial essential-product list.
The calculator estimates TC and does not claim a formal VAT system.
The separate KMF 50-per-minute incoming-call termination additional tax is expressly excluded and never folded into the ad valorem rate.
No filing, deduction, withholding, return or payment decision is automated.
privacy: Calculation, invoice data and PDF remain local; shared links contain no entered values.
tests: Executable AfroVAT fixtures cover missing, mismatched and accepted Article 152 evidence plus browser/API parity.
